title: The Fox and the Grapes
story: A hungry fox saw fine bunches of grapes hanging from a vine trained along a high trellis. He jumped as high as he could but they were just out of reach. After many failed leaps, he gave up, walked away, and remarked that the grapes were probably sour anyway and not worth eating.

title: The Boy Who Cried Wolf
story: A shepherd boy grew bored watching his flock and shouted that a wolf was attacking, just to watch the villagers come running in alarm. He repeated the trick twice more, laughing each time the villagers found no wolf. When a wolf truly came and tore into the flock, his cries for help were ignored, and the sheep were lost.

title: The Monkey and the Crocodile
story: A crocodile befriended a monkey who lived on a fruit tree by the river and began visiting daily. Secretly persuaded by his wife to bring the monkey's heart, the crocodile invited him across the river to meet her. Midway, he confessed his plan. Thinking quickly, the monkey said he had left his heart behind in the tree, and the crocodile rowed him back to fetch it, allowing him to escape to safety.

title: The Tortoise and the Geese
story: A tortoise living in a drying pond asked two geese friends for help finding a new home. The geese agreed to carry him by having him grip a stick in his mouth while they held the ends and flew. They warned him not to speak during the flight. As villagers below marveled and commented on the sight, the tortoise could not resist replying, lost his grip on the stick, and fell to the ground.

title: King Shibi and the Dove
story: A dove fleeing a hunting hawk sought refuge in the lap of King Shibi, who vowed to protect any creature seeking his shelter. The hawk arrived and demanded the dove as its rightful prey, citing the law of the forest. Unwilling to break his word or abandon the dove, King Shibi offered an equal weight of his own flesh in its place, and began cutting his own arm to place on the scale.

title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
story: All summer, an ant worked tirelessly storing food for winter while a grasshopper played music and mocked her efforts, certain that food would always be plentiful. When winter arrived bare and cold, the grasshopper found himself starving with nothing stored, while the ant's burrow remained full from the long months of careful preparation.

title: The Goose That Laid the Golden Eggs
story: A farmer owned a goose that laid one golden egg every day, slowly making him wealthy. Impatient for greater riches all at once, he decided the goose must be full of gold inside and cut it open to take everything immediately. He found nothing but ordinary flesh, and having killed the goose, lost the daily golden eggs forever.

title: The Elephants and the Mice
story: A herd of elephants regularly trampled a path through a colony of mice on their way to a lake, killing many by accident. The mice king pleaded with the elephant king, who agreed to change the herd's route out of respect. Much later, hunters trapped the elephants in nets. Remembering the kindness, the mice gathered in great numbers and gnawed through the ropes, freeing the entire herd.

title: The Cat and the Birds
story: A cat overheard that the birds in a nearby aviary were sick and dressed as a physician, calling at their door to offer his medical help. The birds, peering through the bars, replied that they would be quite well if the doctor simply went away, recognizing that his true intention was to eat them rather than to heal them.

title: The Monkey and the Wedge
story: Carpenters left a half-sawn log with a wedge holding the cut open while they went to lunch. A curious monkey, playing near the worksite, climbed onto the log and pulled out the wedge to see what it did. The log snapped shut on his hands, trapping him painfully until the carpenters returned to find him caught by his own meddling.
